description:
This map displays an interferometric Synthetic Aperture Radar (InSAR) phase image over an unlabelled mountainous terrain. The map includes a compass rose indicating North in the upper-left corner and a 20 km scale bar in the upper-right. A colour bar at the bottom indicates interferometric phase values ranging from -π (blue) through 0 (green) to +π (red). The underlying greyscale image represents topography.
The central part of the image features a distinct pattern of concentric interferometric fringes, which indicates a significant ground displacement component (`φ_d`). Several areas are explicitly labelled "decorrelation" (upper-left, middle-right, and lower-middle), highlighting regions where the interferometric signal has lost coherence. The lower-left portion of the map is labelled "atmospheric effects", with three white arrows pointing to diffuse phase variations attributed to atmospheric perturbations (`φ_a`). The map visually separates the components contributing to the interferometric phase (`φ_i`), distinguishing the displacement signal from other noise sources like decorrelation and atmospheric effects.
